entries.hint_text: A depth compensator module is somewhere in the debris field. You'll need it to go deeper.
entries.location_hint: Wreckage debris field north-east of lifepod, ~50m depth. Look for a yellow equipment crate.

entries.hint_text: Signal detected: partial flight recorder data. Source bearing 220°, estimated 300m.
entries.location_hint: South-west of crash site, partially buried in sand at 30m. Signal pings every 8 seconds.

entries.hint_text: NOA: Biological mass detected nearby. I recommend scanning it before anything else disturbs it.
entries.location_hint: Kelp forest biome, north edge. Large pale ovoid object resting on the seabed at ~80m.

entries.hint_text: Equipment log recovered: high-efficiency swim fins stowed in secondary locker, sector 4.
entries.location_hint: Sunken supply module, east side of crash field. Access panel on the port side, ~45m.

entries.hint_text: NOA: I've recovered a partial memory archive. You should hear this.
entries.location_hint: Accessible directly from the lifepod NOA terminal after initial boot sequence.

entries.hint_text: Structural analysis shows salvageable alloy concentrations near the main hull breach.
entries.location_hint: Inside the Aurora hull breach, first chamber. Three wall-mounted resource caches.

entries.hint_text: NOA: This material composition doesn't match anything in the ship's database. Origin unknown.
entries.location_hint: Grassy plateaus biome, south-east. Partially exposed stone formation at ~120m, bioluminescent markings.
